author: anon-a7c6025f
content: Decision: Writing a two-pass parallel reduction PTX kernel. Pass 1: each block reduces a chunk of input using shared memory + warp shuffle (__shfl_down_sync), writing one partial sum per block. Pass 2: reduce the partial sums to a single scalar. This is the standard CUDA reduction pattern — O(n/blockDim) work per thread, logarithmic reduction within blocks. Target: 256 threads/block.
